title: विष्णु द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्री विष्णुद्वादशनामस्तोत्रस्य
नारद ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः।
श्रीमहाविष्णुर्देवता।
सर्वपापक्षयार्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

तथा च —

प्रथमं केशवं प्रोक्तं द्वितीयं नारायणम्।
तृतीयं माधवं ज्ञेयं चतुर्थं गोविन्दकम्॥१॥

पञ्चमं विष्णुमुद्दिष्टं षष्ठं च मधुसूदनम्।
सप्तमं त्रिविक्रमं प्रोक्तमष्टमं वामनं स्मृतम्॥२॥

नवमं श्रीधरं प्रोक्तं दशमं हृषीकेशम्।
एकादशं पद्मनाभं द्वादशं दामोदरम्॥३॥

द्वादशैतानि नामानि विष्णुप्रोक्तानि भक्तितः।
यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥४॥

॥ द्वादश नामानि ॥
ॐ केशवाय नमः।
ॐ नारायणाय नमः।
ॐ माधवाय नमः।
ॐ गोविन्दाय नमः।
ॐ विष्णवे नमः।
ॐ मधुसूदनाय नमः।
ॐ त्रिविक्रमाय नमः।
ॐ वामनाय नमः।
ॐ श्रीधराय नमः।
ॐ हृषीकेशाय नमः।
ॐ पद्मनाभाय नमः।
ॐ दामोदराय नमः।

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
द्वादशैतानि नामानि त्रिसन्ध्यं यः पठेन्नरः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥

वासनाद् वासुदेवस्य वासितं ते जगत्त्रयम्।
सर्वभूतनिवासोऽसि वासुदेव नमोऽस्तु ते॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Vishnu-dwadasha-nama-stotrasya
Narada Rishiph. Anushtup Chhandah.
Shri Maha-Vishnur-devata.
Sarva-papa-kshayarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Tatha Cha —

Prathamam Keshavam Proktam Dvitiyam Narayanam.
Tritiyam Madhavam Jneyam Chaturtham Govindakam.||1||

Panchamam Vishnum-uddishtam Shashtham Cha Madhusudanam.
Saptamam Trivikramam Proktam-ashtamam Vamanam Smritam.||2||

Navamam Shridharam Proktam Dashamam Hrishikesham.
Ekadasham Padmanabham Dwadasham Damodaram.||3||

Dwadash-aitani Namani Vishnu-proktani Bhaktitah.
Yah Pathet Pratar-utthaya Sarva-papaih Pramuchyate.||4||

|| Dwadash Namani ||
Om Keshavaya Namah.
Om Narayanaya Namah.
Om Madhavaya Namah.
Om Govindaya Namah.
Om Vishnave Namah.
Om Madhusudanaya Namah.
Om Trivikramaya Namah.
Om Vamanaya Namah.
Om Shridharaya Namah.
Om Hrishikeshaya Namah.
Om Padmanabhaya Namah.
Om Damodaraya Namah.

|| Phalashruti ||
Dwadash-aitani Namani Trisandhyam Yah Pathennnarah.
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnulokam Sa Gachchhati.

Vasanad Vasudevasya Vasitam Te Jagattrayam.
Sarvabhuta-nivaso-asi Vasudeva Namostu Te.

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
इस विष्णु द्वादश नाम स्तोत्र के ऋषि नारद हैं, छन्द अनुष्टुप् है, देवता श्री महाविष्णु हैं। सर्वपापक्षय के लिए जप का विनियोग है।

१. प्रथम केशव, द्वितीय नारायण।
२. तृतीय माधव, चतुर्थ गोविन्द।
३. पञ्चम विष्णु, षष्ठ मधुसूदन।
४. सप्तम त्रिविक्रम, अष्टम वामन।
५. नवम श्रीधर, दशम हृषीकेश।
६. एकादश पद्मनाभ, द्वादश दामोदर।

ये बारह नाम विष्णु द्वारा कहे गए हैं। जो प्रातःकाल उठकर भक्तिपूर्वक इन्हें पढ़ता है, वह सर्वपापों से मुक्त हो जाता है।

जो त्रिसन्ध्या (प्रातः, मध्याह्न, सायं) इन बारह नामों का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वपापमुक्त होकर विष्णुलोक जाता है। वासुदेव के वास से ही तीनों लोक व्याप्त हैं, सर्वभूतनिवासी वासुदेव को नमस्कार।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीविष्णुद्वादशनामस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य नारद ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री महाविष्णु देवता। ॐ नमो नारायणाय बीजम्। विष्णु शक्तिः। सर्वपापक्षय कीलकम्। श्रीमहाविष्णुप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Contains the 12 sacred names of Lord Vishnu recited during Sankalpa
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins when recited morning and at three sandhya times
Part of daily Vaishnava worship and essential ritual recitation
Keshava to Damodara — covers 12 months and 12 forms of Vishnu
Invokes vishnu loka and liberation from the cycle of birth and death
Simple yet extremely powerful — can be memorized easily
description: The Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram, known in Sanskrit as विष्णु द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Vishnu. Rooted in Padma Purana / Vishnu Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant Vishnu Loka and liberation from the cycle of birth and death as well as keshava to Damodara — covers 12 months and 12 forms of Vishnu. Additionally, it is known to part of daily Vaishnava worship and essential ritual recitation. This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ekadashi and Vaikuntha Ekadashi to invoke divine grace. Comprising 12 verses, it can be completed in approximately 4 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram and which scripture is it from?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram (विष्णु द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्) is the foundational Vaishnava enumeration of the twelve principal names of Lord Vishnu, taught by Sage Narada and preserved in the Padma Purana, the Vishnu Purana and the Garuda Purana. The viniyog declares: Asya Shri Vishnu Dvadasha Nama Stotrasya Narada Rishih, Anushtup Chhandah, Shri Mahavishnur Devata, Sarva-papa-kshayarthe Jape Viniyogah — the rishi is Narada, the chhandas is Anushtup, the deity is Mahavishnu, and the purpose is the destruction of all sins. The twelve names from Keshava to Damodara constitute the most fundamental Vaishnava nama-japa, recited at every Vaishnava sankalpa, at every Vishnu puja, and as the daily morning prayer of millions. (Source: Padma Purana / Vishnu Purana; Narada upadesha.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the twelve names of Vishnu in their traditional order?
seoFaqs.answer: The twelve names in their canonical order are: (1) Keshava — the long-haired one, slayer of Keshi, (2) Narayana — the shelter of all beings, (3) Madhava — the consort of Ma (Lakshmi), or the lord of vidya (Madhu-Vidya), (4) Govinda — the cowherd, the giver of speech and earth, (5) Vishnu — the all-pervading, (6) Madhusudana — slayer of the demon Madhu, (7) Trivikrama — the conqueror of three worlds in three steps, (8) Vamana — the dwarf incarnation, (9) Shridhara — bearer of Shri (Lakshmi) on his chest, (10) Hrishikesha — lord of the senses, (11) Padmanabha — having a lotus in his navel from which Brahma emerged, (12) Damodara — bound at the waist (by Yashoda's rope). These twelve are recited as Om Keshavaya Namah, Om Narayanaya Namah, etc. (Source: Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram verses 1-3.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are these twelve names recited during sankalpa and at every Vaishnava ritual?
seoFaqs.answer: The twelve names of Vishnu are recited at the very beginning of every Vaishnava ritual — every sankalpa (vow declaration), every puja, every yajna, every samskara (life-cycle ceremony) — for several profound reasons: (1) they consecrate time itself by associating the present moment with all twelve forms of Vishnu pervading the cosmos; (2) they correspond to the twelve months of the Vaishnava year (one name per month, with Keshava ruling Margashirsha in the Vaikhanasa tradition); (3) they purify the speech and the speaker before any mantra is uttered; (4) they invoke Vishnu's complete presence and protection over the ritual to come; (5) the phalashruti specifically promises sarva-papa-kshaya (destruction of all sins) when recited at trisandhya. (Sources: Padma Purana; Vaishnava sankalpa paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how often should the Vishnu Dwadash Nama be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Vishnu Dwadash Nama specifies trisandhyam yah pathennarah — one who recites these twelve names at the three sandhyas (sunrise, midday, sunset) becomes liberated from all sins and attains Vishnuloka. It is most powerful on Thursday (Guruvar — sacred to Vishnu and Brihaspati), every Ekadashi (Vishnu's tithi), every Dvadashi (the twelfth tithi corresponding to the twelve names), Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha-Pushya Shukla Ekadashi), Devshayani Ekadashi, Devuthani Ekadashi, every Vishnu festival (Rama Navami, Krishna Janmashtami, Narasimha Jayanti, Vamana Jayanti), and as the daily nityapaath of every Vaishnava. Pratar-utthaya — chanted immediately on rising in the morning — it grants protection from sin throughout the day. (Source: Vishnu Dwadash Nama phalashruti; Vaishnava sandhya paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the closing verse Vasanad Vasudevasya?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Dwadash Nama concludes with one of the most beloved verses of Vaishnava theology: Vasanad Vasudevasya vasitam te jagat-trayam, Sarvabhuta-nivaso'si Vasudeva namostu te — meaning: O Vasudeva, by your residing (vasanad) the three worlds are filled and inhabited (vasitam te jagat-trayam) — you are the indwelling residence of all beings (sarvabhuta-nivaso'si) — Vasudeva, salutations to you. The verse offers a beautiful etymology of the name Vasudeva — not merely son of Vasudeva, but the One who pervades all (vas — to dwell), the indwelling Self of every being. This is the supreme Vaishnava recognition of Vishnu as the antaryamin — the inner controller of all. (Sources: Vishnu Dwadash Nama; Vishnu Sahasranama; Bhagavad Gita 7.19.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Vishnu Dwadash Nama different from the Vishnu Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are foundational Vaishnava nama-litanies but at vastly different scales and contexts. The Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram contains just twelve names, was taught by Sage Narada, is preserved in the Padma Purana, takes about four minutes to recite, and is the daily essential — the entry-level Vishnu prayer that every Hindu learns first. The Vishnu Sahasranama (preserved in Mahabharata Anushasana Parva chapter 149) contains 1000 names, was taught by Bhishma Pitamaha to Yudhishthira on his bed of arrows, takes about 25-30 minutes to recite, and is the supreme Vaishnava sahasranama for advanced devotees. The Dwadash Nama is recited daily and at every sankalpa; the Sahasranama is recited weekly (especially Saturday or Ekadashi) and on great festivals. (Sources: Padma Purana; Mahabharata Anushasana Parva.)
